frame.admin_layer_role: CBP/ICE appropriations are federal enforcement/detention ledgers — not immigrant lifetime NPV lines unless explicitly allocated by encounters, beds, or legal-status path
frame.legalization_mix: DHS LPR-by-country × admission-class tables decompose Mexico fiscal path (family vs employment) separate from unauthorized surge stock
frame.tension: FY25 CBP budget falls while ICE ERO/custody rises — enforcement cost vector shifts from border interdiction to interior detention/removal

extraction_notes.admin_pdfs: Sourced from dhs/cbp_fy25_budget_justification.pdf and dhs/ice_fy25_budget_justification.pdf (setup-lifetime Round 10 copy to admin/). Dollars in thousands in tables unless noted.
extraction_notes.lpr_xlsx: Staged as plcy_lpr_by_country_of_birth_fy2005_2022.xlsx; canonical bytes at external/origin/ohss/lpr_country_birth_major_class_2005_2022.xlsx. 14 admission-class sheets FY2005–2022.

parameter_claims.population: CBP Operations and Support + PCI + fee accounts, FY2025 President's Budget
parameter_claims.page_ref: Budget Comparison table Total row ($19,764,120 thousands)
parameter_claims.notes: Down from FY2024 annualized CR $21.234B. Table excludes $309M user-fee offset footnote.
65,622 FTE; $656.9M new border-enforcement investments including +250 Border Patrol Agents.

parameter_claims.population: Southwest border illegal entries detected vs interdicted
parameter_claims.notes: 2,151,413 interdicted of 2,846,098 detected. Unnamed: interdiction count used as immigrant stock in fiscal models.

parameter_claims.population: Southwest border unique subjects with repeat encounters
parameter_claims.notes: 168,944 recidivists of 1,423,579 unique subjects. Repeat encounters inflate apprehension flows vs unique-person fiscal stock.

parameter_claims.page_ref: Appropriation and PPA Summary Total ($9,695,379 thousands)
parameter_claims.notes: Narrative rounds to $9.7B; +$556.8M vs FY2024 annualized CR; $691.0M emergency-designated.

parameter_claims.population: Enforcement and Removal Operations PPA FY2025
parameter_claims.notes: FY2024 CR $4.521B. Interior enforcement/removal ledger distinct from CBP border operations.
FY2024 $2.880B (+13.6%). Transportation and Removal Program FY25 $648.6M vs $420.7M FY24.
+25% vs FY2022 (5,659); planning target 34,000 beds; actual ADP 36,804 adults cited in bed-rate footnote.

parameter_claims.page_ref: Average adult bed cost performance measure
parameter_claims.notes: FY2025 budgeted rate $164.65 vs $148.62 FY2023 — fixed-cost share makes ADP inversely related to per-bed rate.

parameter_claims.population: Mexico-born new LPRs, all admission classes summed
parameter_claims.page_ref: All sheets, Mexico country row FY2022 column
parameter_claims.notes: Down from 154,162 in FY2005. Immediate-relative spouses 53,326 + parents 31,691 dominate 2022 mix.
Collapsed from 56,318 in FY2005 — backlog/visa-cap channel, not unauthorized stock. Still #1 country in Fam 2nd 2022.

generators.name: Enforcement budget ≠ per-migrant fiscal cost
generators.prompt: For each CBP/ICE appropriation line, tag as (a) fixed agency overhead, (b) volume-linked (encounters/beds/removals), (c) non-immigration mission (HSI fentanyl, trade). Never divide total CBP $19.8B or ICE $9.7B by immigrant stock without an explicit allocation rule. Cross-check to NAS/CBO lifetime tables that omit enforcement entirely.
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked treating DHS budget growth as direct per-capita immigrant lifetime cost and surfaced NAS enforcement omission.
generators.negative_space: Advocacy and restrictionist frames both treat agency budget as immigrant line-item without encounter/bed denominator.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: CBP budget divided by apprehensions equals cost per immigrant
ICE custody budget maps 1:1 to unauthorized fiscal burden
FY25 CBP cut implies lower border pressure (ICE ERO rose simultaneously)

generators.name: Recidivism-inflated flow vs unique-person stock
generators.prompt: Separate CBP apprehension/encounter counts (2.15M interdicted; 2.81 avg per recidivist) from unique-subject stock (1.42M) and from Pew/DHS unauthorized LPR residual. Where do lifetime models use flow denominators on stock numerators?
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged repeat-encounter inflation before calibrating per-apprehension detention cost or fiscal scalars.
generators.negative_space: Headline apprehension totals treated as unduplicated immigrant arrivals in media and some fiscal shorthand.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Recidivism is negligible for fiscal stock
Title 8 vs Title 42 encounter regimes are pooled
generators.theory_tested: unique_subjects / total_interdictions < 0.55 on SW border FY2023

generators.name: Detention bed-day cost bridge
generators.prompt: Build marginal detention cost from ICE custody PPA ($3.27B FY25) ÷ ADP × 365 vs published bed rate ($187.48/day FY23). Compare to Alternatives to Detention ($360M FY25) and transportation/removal surge (+54% YoY). Which NAS/CBO lifetime paths include interior detention?
generators.retrodiction: Would have quantified interior custody as episodic federal cost absent from state-local K-12/shelter ledgers and NAS education NPV cells.
generators.negative_space: Lifetime NPV studies silent on ICE bed-days while local shock ledgers silent on federal detention.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Per-bed rate is marginal cost (fixed contracts dominate)
Detention days equal removal probability × processing time
generators.theory_tested: custody_ppa / (ADP * 365) approximates bed_rate within fixed-cost band

generators.name: LPR admission-class fiscal path decomposition
generators.prompt: For Mexico and top surge origins (Cuba, Venezuela, Haiti), sum LPR totals by admission class (immediate relative, family preference, employment, diversity). Map family-heavy paths to descendant-inclusive NAS rows vs employment paths to high-skill positive cells. Track Fam 2nd Mexico collapse 2005→2022 as legalization-channel shift under visa caps.
generators.retrodiction: Would have prevented treating Mexico as monolithic <HS unauthorized cell when 2022 LPR flow is 83k+ immediate-relative dominated.
generators.negative_space: Origin labels in ACS fiscal scenarios collapse legal-status and admission-class heterogeneity.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: LPR family paths have same NPV as unauthorized surge
Fam 2nd decline implies lower Mexico immigration (LPR ≠ inflow stock)

generators.name: Border enforcement selection interacts with LPR mix
generators.prompt: Joint read: CBP interdiction rate + ICE ADP growth + Lozano-López border-enforcement selection (Round 9) + Mexico LPR class mix. Does rising interior detention correlate with positive earnings selection on observables while LPR family backlog lengthens?
generators.retrodiction: Would have linked Round 9 IZA dp4898 selection story to admin budget shift border→interior and LPR Fam2 collapse.
generators.negative_space: Enforcement papers treat selection; budget docs treat dollars; LPR tables treat legal paths — rarely joint.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: More enforcement uniformly lowers immigrant quality
Selection parameters from Mexico apply to Venezuela parole surge

theories_tested.theory: Interior detention spend dominates marginal federal enforcement cost per unique SW-border entrant
theories_tested.prediction: ICE custody PPA ($3.27B) / (unique SW subjects ~1.42M) >> CBP Border Patrol PPA per interdiction ($6.03B / 2.15M) when recidivism deduplicated
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Register J-006–J-010 as external priors; JOIN origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 avg_federal_net (excludes detention) WITH state_stage5_context_2023 for scale — compare implied federal proxy to ICE custody PPA / Pew unauthorized stock
theories_tested.falsifier: Deduped per-person ICE+C BP enforcement cost < |NAS <HS individual NPV| without double-counting

theories_tested.theory: Mexico LPR legalization path shifted from family-preference backlog to immediate-relative dominance (2022)
theories_tested.prediction: immediate_relative_lpr_2022 / total_mexico_lpr_2022 > 0.75 while fam2_mexico_lpr fell >60% vs 2005
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Load plcy_lpr xlsx to DuckDB; aggregate Mexico rows by class; JOIN origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 Mexico row — flag if scenario treats origin as unauthorized-only
theories_tested.falsifier: Employment/diversity classes exceed 25% of Mexico LPR 2022 or total Mexico LPR rises with Fam2

theories_tested.theory: Receiver-city shelter shocks are fiscally separate from federal enforcement appropriations (no automatic offset)
theories_tested.prediction: receiver_city_migrant_costs NYC FY2024 ($3.7B) > annual Mexico federal proxy (~$664M from origin scenario) while ICE+CBP budgets omit local shelter
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Compare receiver_city_migrant_costs total_spending_usd_M to origin_fiscal_scenario_2023 (weighted_adults * avg_federal_net) for surge-origin labels; cross state_stage5_context_2023 RPP/medicaid for host-state capacity
theories_tested.falsifier: Sum of federal enforcement appropriations allocated to NYC asylum spend fully covers receiver_city costs without double-counting federal proxy
